profile.name: Digvijay Kumar Singh
profile.tagline: I ship full-stack AI products from schema to deployment. Four live. Zero hand-holding.
profile.intro: Four production applications. A 37-table PostgreSQL schema. AI wired to real user flows — not demo endpoints. I own the complete stack: data model, REST API, AI integration, frontend, and deployment. No handoffs, no gaps. My work spans an autonomous AI career OS, a real-time multi-tenant workspace with subscription billing, a job tracker with a custom scraper that imports from any job board in one click, and an AI SaaS platform with API-enforced access control. I don't stop at "working locally."
profile.availability: Available for full-stack SDE-1 roles and remote contracts worldwide. I work async, communicate clearly, and deliver on time.
journey: Skipped the tutorial treadmill entirely. Learned React, Node.js, Express, and MongoDB by shipping real applications with real deployment pipelines — because that's the only practice that actually transfers.

Shipped three production SaaS products back-to-back: an AI job tracker, a multi-tenant real-time workspace with billing, and an AI chat SaaS with API-level access control. Each one went to production with auth, payments, and AI working together.

Pushed the stack forward — PostgreSQL with Supabase, TanStack Start SSR, multi-model AI routing via OpenRouter and Groq, N8N workflow automation. All applied directly in Valtrexa V2, the most architecturally complex system I've designed.
focusAreas: AI integration with Groq and OpenRouter
production SaaS patterns — auth, billing, RBAC
REST API design and multi-platform deployment
TanStack and modern React application structure

highlights.label: shipped
highlights.note: MERN · Next.js · Supabase · AI APIs — all in production

highlights.label: building now
highlights.note: Autonomous AI career OS · 37-table schema · multi-model AI routing

timeline.title: Rebuilt my practice around shipping, not studying
timeline.description: Dropped isolated tutorials for end-to-end product builds. Every technology — React, Node.js, Express, MongoDB — learned by deploying something real with a live URL and a working backend.

timeline.title: InternPe internship + three SaaS products shipped to production
timeline.description: Completed a frontend development internship while simultaneously shipping three full-stack SaaS products — each with auth, third-party integrations, AI features, and live deployment. Not demos. Products.

timeline.title: Expanded into PostgreSQL, TanStack, and multi-model AI architecture
timeline.description: Moved beyond MERN into relational schema design with Supabase, server-side rendering with TanStack Start, and multi-model AI routing with OpenRouter and Groq — all applied hands-on in Valtrexa V2.

timeline.title: Finishing Valtrexa V2 and targeting full-stack roles
timeline.description: Finalizing a 37-table AI career OS with autonomous workflows — the hardest thing I've built. Sharpening DSA, deepening API architecture, and looking for the right team to build with next.

projects.name: Valtrexa V2
projects.label: AI career operating system
projects.summary: An autonomous AI career OS that replaces a dozen scattered tabs with one system — job tracking, recruiter CRM, resume intelligence, outreach automation, and interview prep, all connected by a 37-table PostgreSQL schema.
projects.description: Job searching is fragmented by design: résumés in Docs, contacts in a spreadsheet, follow-ups forgotten in your inbox. Valtrexa V2 is the fix — a single intelligent system that owns the entire career workflow, with AI handling the repetitive work autonomously while you focus on the conversations that matter.
projects.challenge: Design a deeply normalized relational schema, a multi-model AI routing layer, and an event-driven automation architecture — all while building with a stack (TanStack Start, Supabase, OpenRouter, N8N) that was new territory during the build itself.
projects.solution: 37-table PostgreSQL schema in Supabase with row-level security. TanStack Start SSR with 15+ structured views. Multi-model AI routing via OpenRouter and Groq with a free-tier fallback chain and per-request token tracking. PDF/DOCX parsing, Cheerio and Playwright scraping, and an event-driven architecture wired into N8N for autonomous workflow orchestration — controlled through a Telegram command interface.
highlights: 37-table normalized PostgreSQL schema with Supabase row-level security
multi-model AI routing via OpenRouter and Groq with per-request token cost tracking
TanStack Start SSR — 15+ structured views across job tracking, CRM, resume, and interview prep
provider abstraction for Greenhouse, Lever, LinkedIn, and Wellfound job import
N8N event-driven workflow automation with Telegram command interface
PDF/DOCX parsing pipeline with pdfkit, pdf-parse, and mammoth
frontendDetails: React 19 with TanStack Start — file-based SSR and structured route-based architecture
TanStack Query for server state, Zustand for client state
full Radix UI component library with react-hook-form and Zod validation
15+ views across job tracking, recruiter CRM, resume management, and interview prep
backendDetails: Supabase-managed PostgreSQL with RLS for multi-tenant data isolation
ai_generations table with per-request token and cost tracking
OpenRouter multi-model routing with free-tier fallback chain on rate limits
Cheerio and Playwright scraping pipeline with Google APIs and PDF/DOCX processing
architectureHighlights: event-driven architecture emitting workflow events into N8N for autonomous processing
provider abstraction layer supporting Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby, LinkedIn, and Wellfound
TypeScript 5 strict mode throughout with Supabase-generated type definitions
Telegram HQ command interface for real-time operational control

projects.name: JobPilot AI
projects.label: AI job application tracker
projects.summary: A full-stack AI job tracker with a universal web scraper that imports from any job board in one click, a drag-and-drop Kanban pipeline, Groq-powered follow-up generation, and automated cron reminders.
projects.description: Job searches die in browser tabs. JobPilot AI eliminates that: paste any job listing URL and it auto-fills the details — title, company, location, salary — using a custom Cheerio scraper with no site-specific config. Track every application in a Kanban board, get AI-written follow-ups, and receive automated reminders before you go quiet.
projects.challenge: Build a job importer that works across every job board without per-site configuration, wire it to AI follow-ups, automated cron reminders, and a Kanban board — without making the interface feel overloaded.
projects.solution: Cheerio DOM parser with a 4-stage waterfall — itemprop → OpenGraph → CSS heuristics → page title fallback. Groq (llama-3.3-70b-versatile) for follow-up email generation. DnD Kit Kanban with Redux Toolkit position persistence. node-cron 15-minute reminder sweep with HTML email delivery via Nodemailer. Express 5 with native ESM, Swagger/OpenAPI docs, and JWT + Google OAuth auth.
highlights: Cheerio web scraper — imports job fields from any URL using itemprop, OpenGraph, and CSS heuristics, no site-specific config
Groq (llama-3.3-70b) for AI follow-up email generation and job summarization
drag-and-drop Kanban pipeline with DnD Kit and Redux Toolkit position persistence
node-cron automated reminders — 15-minute sweep with HTML email delivery via Nodemailer
Express 5 with native ESM modules, Swagger/OpenAPI docs, and asyncHandler pattern
JWT + Google OAuth 2.0 with bcrypt hashing and middleware-protected routes
frontendDetails: Next.js 14 with TypeScript — structured application routing and SSR page loads
DnD Kit drag-and-drop Kanban across Applied, Interview, Offer, and Rejected columns
shadcn/ui component system with Radix primitives and Tailwind CSS
Redux Toolkit for Kanban state persistence and cross-view user context
backendDetails: Express 5 with native ESM modules, asyncHandler middleware, and Swagger/OpenAPI docs
Cheerio DOM parser with 4-stage extraction waterfall and per-field sanitization
Groq API integration with 90-second timeout, structured prompts, and HTTP error propagation
node-cron 15-minute reminder sweep with MongoDB-indexed queries and Nodemailer delivery
architectureHighlights: job scraper designed for any URL — no site-specific configuration required
Express 5 ESM backend deployed on Render with CORS-locked production config
MongoDB user-indexed queries to avoid full collection scans in cron sweeps
Cloudinary resume uploads with signed presets alongside Swagger-documented API

projects.name: DsSync Hub
projects.label: multi-tenant team collaboration SaaS
projects.summary: A multi-tenant SaaS workspace with Socket.io real-time chat, Groq AI task intelligence, Razorpay subscription billing, and a 16-model MongoDB schema — tasks, notes, channels, and meetings in one focused system.
projects.description: Most collaboration tools are either too simple or too spread across separate apps. DsSync Hub is a focused workspace that covers tasks, notes, real-time chat, meetings, and billing — all scoped per workspace, with AI turning free-text into structured tasks and subscription tiers controlling feature access.
projects.challenge: Build a cohesive multi-feature SaaS product where real-time chat, AI intelligence, and billing coexist — without the architecture becoming fragmented or data boundaries becoming inconsistent across workspace tenants.
projects.solution: 16 MongoDB models for workspace-scoped multi-tenant data. Socket.io room-scoped real-time chat with typing indicators. Groq for free-text to structured task conversion and chat thread summarization. Razorpay webhooks with plan enforcement middleware that works at the API level. JWT + Google OAuth with Nodemailer-driven email verification and password reset.
highlights: 16-model MongoDB schema — workspaces, channels, tasks, notes, meetings with workspace-scoped isolation
Socket.io real-time chat with room-scoped message delivery and live typing indicators
Groq AI — free-text to structured tasks, chat thread summarization, draft rewriting
Razorpay subscription billing with webhook handling and API-level plan enforcement
per-user AI usage-limit middleware — gated in the backend, not just the UI
JWT + Google OAuth 2.0 with email verification and password reset via Nodemailer
frontendDetails: React and TypeScript client with module-oriented component structure
Redux Toolkit for shared auth state, workspace context, and cross-view data
Tailwind-based SaaS interface across dashboard, billing, notes, chat, and task views
socket-aware components for real-time message updates and typing feedback
backendDetails: Express API with 16 Mongoose models and workspace-scoped data isolation
Socket.io WebSocket layer for room-scoped real-time chat and typing events
Groq integration with per-user usage-limit middleware for controlled AI access
Razorpay webhooks, JWT sessions, Google OAuth, email verification, and CORS-locked deployment
architectureHighlights: 16-model MongoDB schema designed for workspace-scoped, role-based data access
real-time chat via Socket.io alongside REST API for all other product operations
Groq AI gated by per-user middleware — not just UI-level checks
separate Vercel frontend and Render backend with MongoDB Atlas connection pooling

projects.name: DevFlow AI
projects.label: AI SaaS chat platform
projects.summary: A full-stack AI chat SaaS where RBAC and usage limits are enforced at the API level — not just hidden in the UI. Groq-powered chat, Razorpay billing tiers, and Cloudinary media handling.
projects.description: DevFlow AI is an exercise in SaaS access control done correctly. Usage limits and role checks live in Express middleware — which means they can't be bypassed by manipulating the frontend. Free plan limits, upgrade flows, and account controls all behave as expected because the API enforces them, not the React component.
projects.challenge: Build RBAC and per-account usage limits that are actually enforced in the backend — not just hidden behind frontend conditionals — while keeping the AI chat experience clean and the upgrade path frictionless.
projects.solution: Express middleware with admin/user role checks and per-account daily usage limits that hold even if the frontend is bypassed. Groq AI chat with MongoDB-backed session history. Razorpay plan upgrades with server-side payment verification. Cloudinary signed uploads. Separate Netlify (Next.js) and Render (Express) deployment with HTTPS CORS allowlists.
highlights: RBAC with admin/user roles enforced in Express middleware — not UI gating
per-account usage limits enforced in the backend — correct even if the frontend is bypassed
Groq AI chat with persistent session history across MongoDB
Razorpay billing with server-side payment verification and plan upgrade logic
Cloudinary media handling with signed upload presets
Separate Netlify + Render deployment with HTTPS CORS allowlists
frontendDetails: Next.js frontend organized around chat, billing, account settings, and plan management
Redux for plan-aware user state and feature access control in the UI layer
Tailwind-based responsive interface with chat and account-focused views
subscription tier display and Razorpay-connected upgrade flow
backendDetails: Express API with RBAC middleware — admin and user roles with per-account usage limit enforcement
Groq AI chat with MongoDB-backed message persistence and session history
Razorpay payment verification with plan upgrade logic and subscription record updates
Cloudinary signed upload presets and environment-managed secrets on Render
architectureHighlights: usage limits enforced in Express middleware — correct even if the frontend is bypassed
separate Netlify (Next.js) and Render (Express) deployment with HTTPS CORS allowlists
RBAC implementation demonstrates security-first API design beyond typical project work
Groq chat integrated into a broader SaaS billing structure — not an isolated feature

contact.heading: Let's build something that ships.
contact.description: If you need a developer who takes a product from idea to deployed implementation — schema, API, AI, and UI all included — I'm available for full-stack SDE-1 roles, remote contracts, and freelance projects starting now. I work async, communicate in plain English, and don't disappear mid-project.
